data.title: Uwharrie Bank reports $1.17B in assets, up from $1.13B, with increasing net income for the first half of 2025.
data.description: Albemarle-based Uwharrie Capital Corp and its subsidiary, Uwharrie Bank, announced their consolidated total assets stood at $1.17 billion as of June 30, 2025, marking an increase from $1.13 billion recorded at the end of December 31, 2024. data.title: Boston Teamsters and coalition demand AV regulation for Waymo at City Hall, calling for accountability; joined by elected officials.
data.description: A group consisting of Teamsters, a coalition of unions, and various elected officials gathered at City Hall in Boston on July 25, 2025. Their aim was to push for accountability measures for Autonomous Vehicle (AV) companies like Waymo before allowing them to operate freely within the city. The coalition stressed the importance of regulating these companies to ensure public safety and proper oversight. Key figures in attendance included representatives from different unions and a number of city officials. The call for action comes amid growing concerns surrounding the deployment of AVs in urban areas.

data.title: Spacelift to host IaCConf Spotlight virtual event on IaC Security & Governance. Call for Presenters closes August 1.
data.description: The deadline for presenter applications is approaching on August 1st in REDWOOD CITY, California, as per news released on July 25, 2025. Spacelift is calling for presenters for IaCConf Spotlight: IaC Security & Governance, a virtual event that follows the success of the first IaCConf. This free event will center on tackling the shifting obstacles in securing and overseeing infrastructure as code.
OPEC Clarifies That The Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee Meeting On Monday Does Not Have The Power To Make Decisions About Oil Production Levels

data.title: Ohio Valley Banc Corp reported Q2 2025 net income of $4.21M (41.7% increase), EPS $0.89. Six-month net income was $8.62M (49.5% increase). Total assets $1.51B. Shareholders' equity increased $10.4M. Loan balances grew by $39M. Net interest income increased by $2.57M for Q2 and $4.52M for six months. Non-performing loans ratio was.45%. Non-interest expense increased by $186K for Q2 and $263K for six months. Net interest margin improved to 4.01% for six months. Provision for credit losses increased to $1.56M for six months.
data.description: Ohio Valley Banc Corp. (OVBC) reported a consolidated net income of $4.21 million for Q2 2025, up by $1.24 million from Q2 2024. Earnings per share increased to $0.89 from $0.63 in the previous year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income was $8.61 million, a 49.5% rise from the same period in 2024. Return on average assets and equity were 1.16% and 11.30%, respectively. The company attributed the growth to participating in the Ohio Homebuyer Plus Program and focusing on commercial lending. Net interest income increased due to growth in earning assets and margin. Noninterest income also saw a rise, especially from interchange income on debit/credit cards. Noninterest expenses increased slightly, with total assets at $1.51 billion. Shareholders' equity increased by $10.4 million from the previous year. Loan balances grew, particularly in commercial and residential real estate. The net interest margin improved to 4.17% in Q2 2025. The provision for credit loss expense increased due to loan growth and charge-offs. Nonperforming loans and allowance for credit losses also showed improvements. Overall, OVBC displayed strong financial performance and strategic focus on key lending segments.

data.title: Stock of Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE: EW) surged after Q2 earnings beat, with $1.53B sales (11.9% growth). TAVR sales $1.1B (8.9% increase). surgIt also beat adjusted earnings at 67 cents per share against the consensus of 62 cents and raised full-year guidance, showing positive outlook.
data.description: Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE: EW) saw a surge in their stock following a robust second-quarter 2025 earnings report. Sales reached $1.53 billion, surpassing expectations and growing by 11.9% (10.6% adjusted). Adjusted earnings of 67 cents per share also exceeded expectations. Key growth areas included Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) sales at $1.1 billion, up 8.9%, and Transcatheter Mitral and Tricuspid Therapies (TMTT) sales at $134.5 million, up 61.9% year-over-year. The Surgical segment saw global sales reach $267 million, up 7.7%. The company raised its full-year sales guidance, indicating strong momentum in its core markets. This performance indicates significant growth and positive outlook for Edwards Lifesciences.

data.title: iSpecimen Inc. closed $4M underwritten offering, selling 5.7M shares at $0.70 per share, raising funds for ops.
data.description: In Woburn, Massachusetts, on July 25, 2025, iSpecimen Inc. (Nasdaq: ISPC) revealed the conclusion of its recent underwritten public offering. The offering involved 5,714,283 shares of common stock at a price of $0.70 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of around $4 million for the Company, excluding underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses. iSpecimen operates as a global online platform connecting researchers in need of biospecimens for medical studies with a diverse range of healthcare specimen suppliers.

data.title: Entergy's board declares $0.60 per share quarterly dividend payable Sept. 2, 2025; shareholders must be recorded by Aug. 13, 2025.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, the board of directors of Entergy announced a quarterly dividend of $0.60 per share for its common stock. Shareholders of record as of August 13, 2025, will receive the payment on September 2, 2025. This decision continues Entergy's commitment to providing value to its investors through cash dividends.

data.title: Inventor from Oswego, Kan creates SAFE DRINKING modification for bottles to indicate drinking while driving at night.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 25, 2025, an inventor from Oswego, Kansas shared the concept behind his creation, SAFE DRINKING. The inventor aimed to develop a method to alter beer and liquor bottles to indicate if they are being lifted for consumption while driving at night. This innovative solution is designed to help reduce instances of drinking and driving.

data.title: Galactic partners with FCM to enhance its US presence and regulatory compliance, boosting market footprint.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in London, Galactic, a prominent player in the prediction markets sector, revealed its plans to strengthen its operations in the United States by partnering with a Futures Commission Merchant (FCM). This move is aimed at bolstering its regulatory compliance efforts and expanding its US footprint.

data.title: Piper Sandler gives Palantir an overweight rating and $170 price target, ignoring high valuation. Stock soars.
data.description: Piper Sandler recently began covering Palantir stock, giving it an overweight rating and setting a price target of $170, despite its high valuation. This move comes as Palantir's stock hits a new high.

data.title: SS&C Technologies stock upgraded with improving price performance, earning higher IBD rating.
data.description: SS&C Technologies has displayed improved price performance, leading to an upgrade in its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade signifies positive movement in the company's stock performance. It highlights the growing confidence in SS&C Technologies among investors and analysts, reflecting its strengthening position in the market. This development indicates that SS&C Technologies is on a positive trajectory with potential for further growth and success. Investors may view this upgrade as a favorable signal regarding the company's financial health and prospects, suggesting a promising outlook for the stock.

data.title: United Rentals stock gains with Relative Strength Rating surpassing 80 level, per Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: United Rentals has experienced an increase in its Relative Strength Rating, reaching a level exceeding 80. This upgrade in the stock's rating is significant and indicates positive performance. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Imperial Metals Corp reports safe return of 3 workers at Red Chris Mine in NW British Columbia after fall incident.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on July 25, 2025, Imperial Metals Corporation updated that at the Red Chris mine in northwest British Columbia, three workers were trapped underground due to a fall of ground incident. The mine operator, Newmont, reported that at around 10:40 pm PST, the workers - Kevin Coumbs, Darien Maduke, and Jesse Chubaty from Hy-Tech Drilling - were successfully rescued and brought to the surface. Imperial Metals Corporation, identified as III on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), disclosed this incident.
